title: The End of Evangelion poster translated
display_title: The End of Evangelion poster translated
wikitext:
[]: The End of Evangelion poster translated             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Mon Oct 05, 2020 6:41 am Someone translated this poster for The End of Evangelion and I thought I'd share it here. I'm aware that another translation has been shared in these forums, but that was in 2006 and I personally think the design for this one is quite better. Not to mention that many people here aren't probably interested in jumbling through the long history of this forum to find it. Maybe we could even have some discussion based on this. I'm quite intrigued by the list here. ''Ocean of hopelessness'', ''cruel others'','' replacement of opposite sex'', ''immediate cure'' etc. It seems like oblique references to the plot of the film and more generally the Instrumentality parts. There's even ''return to the womb'', showing that Anno really did intend all these Freudian similarities when it comes to the Evas. (Since Shinji and Asuka are essentially returning to their mothers' wombs every time they enter an Eva. This most likely means Shinji and mankind entering Rei/Lilith too, of course, given how she's the progenitor of humanity. Shinji and Unit-01 even enter into her through a rather suspiciously vagina-like third eye while being crucified to a cross which does look like a very crude penis if looked at with the right mindset, upon which they're greeted by dozens of Reis bustling about, obviously a sperm reference. Lovely. The entire Komm, Süsser Tod sequence is basically a giant sex metaphor like this.) It even ends with Shinji's famous question ''is it okay for me to be here?''.

[]: Re: The End of Evangelion poster translated             Quote Post by dzzthink » Sat Oct 10, 2020 11:31 am I think I am right that in the film 'wouldn't it be better if everyone died' was said by Shinji and that 'Then, why are you here' was the response from Rei. Also 'this is at the end of you dream' is also featured in the following exchange: Shinji "But where is my reality" Rei "It is at the end of your dream" It seems as though the reasons for why 'people should just die' is listed below as the painful experiences of modern reality (from the perspective of Shinji). This is then separated by the encouraging wisdom supplied by Rei: 'reality can only be found at the end of your dreams' and ' existence is why you are here'. This gap seems to represent the back and forth exchange between Shinji and Rei that we see a lot in EoE. Simiarly, we get the same gap in the evangelion 3.0+1.0 poster: 'after that and the end... not and anti', which does not seem like dialogue between two people, but there could be all kinds of crazy meta references during intrumentality. Another interpretation could be that 'THE END' and 'NOT and ANTI' are title cards that flash on the screen to separate two different parts of the story, just like how in the EoE poster and movie there are 'episode 25' and 'episode 26'. Just when you think it is the end, it is not and the story continues. Maybe there could be a 'bye-bye all of evangelion' title card at the end to round everything up. So was this poster before or after the release of the movie? Could be that much of the imagery in these posters will end up in the final movie. I'm yet again trying to make predictions for what might turn up in the evangelion 4.0 since we know that the eiffel tower is obviously in the teasers and the three characters wondering in the wastelands in the other poster, but maybe the train tracks could be included as well?

[]: Re: The End of Evangelion poster translated             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Thu Oct 15, 2020 10:11 am dzzthink wrote: It seems as though the reasons for why 'people should just die' is listed below as the painful experiences of modern reality (from the perspective of Shinji).
